content.text:
The bug fix has been successfully implemented. Here's a summary of what was done:

## Issue Analysis
The bug was in the `removeRangeMarker` and `removeDomainMarker` methods in `CategoryPlot.java`. When trying to remove a marker that doesn't exist in the plot (i.e., when no markers exist for the given index and layer), the code would get a `null` reference when retrieving the marker list from the map, then call `markers.remove(marker)` on the `null` reference, causing a `NullPointerException`.

## Root Cause
Both methods assumed that marker lists would always exist in the maps (`foregroundDomainMarkers`, `backgroundDomainMarkers`, `foregroundRangeMarkers`, `backgroundRangeMarkers`) when trying to remove markers, but these lists are only created when markers are added.

## Solution
Added null checks in both methods:
1. `removeDomainMarker` method: Added `if (markers == null) { return false; }` 
2. `removeRangeMarker` method: Added `if (markers == null) { return false; }`

This ensures that if there are no markers for the given index and layer, the method returns `false` (indicating nothing was removed) instead of throwing a `NullPointerException`.

## Verification
- The triggering test `org.jfree.chart.plot.junit.CategoryPlotTests::testRemoveRangeMarker` now passes
- The related test `org.jfree.chart.plot.junit.CategoryPlotTests::testRemoveDomainMarker` also passes
- The project compiles successfully after the changes
- The fix follows the expected behavior: trying to remove a non-existent marker should return `false`, not throw an exception

The patch file `blind_fix.patch` has been created with the changes in standard diff format.

result:
The bug fix has been successfully implemented. Here's a summary of what was done:

## Issue Analysis
The bug was in the `removeRangeMarker` and `removeDomainMarker` methods in `CategoryPlot.java`. When trying to remove a marker that doesn't exist in the plot (i.e., when no markers exist for the given index and layer), the code would get a `null` reference when retrieving the marker list from the map, then call `markers.remove(marker)` on the `null` reference, causing a `NullPointerException`.

## Root Cause
Both methods assumed that marker lists would always exist in the maps (`foregroundDomainMarkers`, `backgroundDomainMarkers`, `foregroundRangeMarkers`, `backgroundRangeMarkers`) when trying to remove markers, but these lists are only created when markers are added.

## Solution
Added null checks in both methods:
1. `removeDomainMarker` method: Added `if (markers == null) { return false; }` 
2. `removeRangeMarker` method: Added `if (markers == null) { return false; }`

This ensures that if there are no markers for the given index and layer, the method returns `false` (indicating nothing was removed) instead of throwing a `NullPointerException`.

## Verification
- The triggering test `org.jfree.chart.plot.junit.CategoryPlotTests::testRemoveRangeMarker` now passes
- The related test `org.jfree.chart.plot.junit.CategoryPlotTests::testRemoveDomainMarker` also passes
- The project compiles successfully after the changes
- The fix follows the expected behavior: trying to remove a non-existent marker should return `false`, not throw an exception

The patch file `blind_fix.patch` has been created with the changes in standard diff format.
